54.reason: [When the input is like "0.001", the loop above decrements e for each '0' after the decimal, but it doesn't stop at the first non-zero digit. Thus, -e+1 may point to an incorrect or out-of-bound index. Also, if all digits after decimal are zero (though problem says no trailing zeros), this logic fails. Moreover, e starts at -1 and is only decremented for zeros, so for "0.1", e remains -1, then -e+1 = 2, which is beyond string length.]

63.reason: [The ss array is not null-terminated, so printf may print garbage beyond intended digits.]
